segments.text: Ahab told Jezebel everything that Elijah had done and that he had killed all the prophets of Baal with the sword.
Jezebel sent a messenger to Elijah to say,
May the gods do as much to me and more if by tomorrow I haven't made your life like the lives of those you killed!
segments.speaker: Jezebel

segments.text: Elijah was afraid and ran for his life. When he arrived in Beersheba in Judah, he left his servant there
and traveled another day's journey into the desert. He sat down under a broom tree and asked to die.
I've had enough now, Lord,
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: he said.
Take my life! I'm no better than my forefathers.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: He lay down and fell asleep under the broom tree. All of a sudden an angel touched him and said,
Get up, and eat.
segments.speaker: An angel

segments.text: He looked around, and there beside his head was some bread baking over hot coals, and a jar of water. He ate and drank and lay down again.
The angel of the Lord returned a second time and touched him, and said,
Get up and eat, otherwise the journey will be too much for you.
segments.speaker: Angel of the LORD

segments.text: So he got up and ate and drank, and with the strength the food gave him he was able to walk forty days and forty nights to Mount Horeb,  the mountain of God.
There he entered a cave and spent the night. The Lord spoke to Elijah, and asked him,
What are you doing here, Elijah?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I have worked passionately for the Lord God Almighty,
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: he replied.
But the Israelites have abandoned your agreement, torn down your altars, and killed your prophets with the sword. I am the only one who's left, and they are trying to kill me as well.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Then the Lord told him,
Go out and st and on the mountain before the Lord.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Right then the Lord passed by. A tremendously powerful wind ripped into the mountains and smashed rocks before the Lord, but the Lord wasn't in the wind. After the wind there came an earthquake, but the Lord wasn't in the earthquake.
After the earthquake there came a fire, but the Lord wasn't in the fire. And after the fire came a voice speaking in a gentle whisper.
When Elijah heard it, he wrapped his cloak around his face and went out and stood at the cave entrance. Immediately a voice spoke to him and asked,
What are you doing here, Elijah?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I have worked passionately for the Lord God Almighty,
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: he replied.
But the Israelites have abandoned your agreement, torn down your altars, and killed your prophets with the sword. I am the only one who's left, and they are trying to kill me as well.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: The Lord told him,
Go back the way you came to the desert of Damascus. When you get there, go and anoint Hazael king of Aram.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Also anoint Jehu, son of Nimshi, king of Israel and Elisha, son of Shaphat, from Abel-meholah, to take over from you as prophet.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Jehu will execute anyone who escapes the sword of Hazael, and Elisha will execute anyone who escapes the sword of Jehu.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I still have seven thousand left in Israel, all those who have not bowed their knees to worship Baal and whose mouths have not kissed him.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: So Elijah left, and went and found Elisha, son of Shaphat. He was plowing with twelve pairs of oxen, and he was with the twelfth pair. Elijah went over to him and threw his cloak around him.
Elisha left the oxen, ran after Elijah, and said,
Please let me go and kiss my father and mother goodbye, and then I will follow you.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Go on home,
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Elijah replied.
I've never done anything for you.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Elisha left him, took his pair of oxen, and slaughtered them. Using the wood of the oxen's yoke as fuel, he cooked the meat and gave it to the people, and they ate it.  Then he left to follow and serve Elijah.
